source: Evidence-based fundamentals from emotion regulation research
description: Essential strategies from Gross Process Model and clinical research

strategies.name: 4-7-8 Breathing Technique
strategies.description: Breathe in for 4 counts, hold for 7, exhale for 8. Activates parasympathetic nervous system to reduce arousal.
steps: Find a comfortable seated position
Place tongue behind upper front teeth
Exhale completely through mouth making whoosh sound
Close mouth and inhale quietly through nose for 4 counts
Hold breath for 7 counts
Exhale completely through mouth for 8 counts making whoosh sound
Repeat cycle 4 times total
Return to normal breathing

citations.author: Weil, A.
citations.title: Breathing: The Master Key to Self Healing

strategies.name: 5-4-3-2-1 Grounding Technique
strategies.description: Engage all five senses to anchor in the present moment and reduce rumination.
steps: Acknowledge 5 things you can SEE around you
Acknowledge 4 things you can TOUCH (feel textures)
Acknowledge 3 things you can HEAR
Acknowledge 2 things you can SMELL
Acknowledge 1 thing you can TASTE

strategies.name: Progressive Muscle Relaxation
strategies.description: Systematically tense and release muscle groups to reduce physical tension and arousal.
steps: Find comfortable position, close eyes
Tense feet/toes for 5 seconds, then release
Tense calves for 5 seconds, then release
Tense thighs for 5 seconds, then release
Tense stomach for 5 seconds, then release
Tense hands/fists for 5 seconds, then release
Tense arms for 5 seconds, then release
Tense shoulders for 5 seconds, then release
Tense face for 5 seconds, then release
Notice the difference between tension and relaxation

strategies.name: Cognitive Reappraisal: Probability Estimation
strategies.description: Challenge catastrophic thinking by estimating actual probability of feared outcomes.
steps: Write down the specific worry or fear
Ask: What's the WORST that could realistically happen? (estimate probability 1-100%)
Ask: What's the BEST that could happen? (estimate probability 1-100%)
Ask: What's MOST LIKELY to happen? (estimate probability 1-100%)
List evidence supporting each scenario
Review: How have similar situations turned out before?
If worst happens, how would you cope? List 3-5 coping strategies

strategies.name: Perspective-Taking: Friend's Advice
strategies.description: Distance from the situation by imagining what you'd tell a friend.
steps: Describe the situation as if it happened to a close friend
What would you tell them?
What compassionate advice would you give?
How would you reassure them?
Now apply that same compassion to yourself
Notice the difference in self-talk

citations.author: Kross, E. & Ayduk, O.
citations.title: Making Meaning out of Negative Experiences

strategies.name: Self-Compassion Break (Kristin Neff)
strategies.description: Three-part self-compassion practice for moments of suffering.
steps: Acknowledge: 'This is a moment of suffering' or 'This hurts'
Common Humanity: 'Suffering is part of life' or 'I'm not alone in this'
Self-Kindness: Place hand on heart and say 'May I be kind to myself'
Optional: 'May I give myself the compassion I need'
Breathe with the difficulty for a few moments
Notice any softening or shift in perspective

citations.author: Neff, K.D.
citations.title: Self-Compassion: The Proven Power of Being Kind to Yourself

strategies.contraindications: May feel uncomfortable at first if unfamiliar with self-compassion

strategies.name: Shame Resilience: Speak Shame
strategies.description: Brené Brown's practice of speaking shame to a trusted person to reduce its power.
steps: Identify someone safe (therapist, close friend, partner)
Name the shame trigger: 'I feel ashamed about...'
Share with the trusted person
Allow them to witness without judgment
Notice shame's power decrease when spoken
Remember: Shame cannot survive empathy
strategies.contraindications: Requires a truly safe, non-judgmental person

strategies.name: Mindfulness Meditation (Basic)
strategies.description: Focus attention on breath to anchor in present and reduce rumination.
steps: Sit comfortably with eyes closed or soft gaze
Notice the sensation of breathing
When mind wanders (it will!), gently return to breath
Don't judge the wandering - it's natural
Start with 5 minutes, build up gradually
Remember: You can't stop thoughts, just notice them

strategies.name: Nature Exposure
strategies.description: Spend time in natural settings to reduce stress and increase well-being.
steps: Go outside to a natural setting (park, trail, garden)
Leave phone behind or on airplane mode
Walk slowly, noticing surroundings
Engage senses: what do you see, hear, smell, feel?
Spend at least 20 minutes
Notice effect on mood and arousal

strategies.name: Reach Out: Text or Call
strategies.description: Combat loneliness by initiating connection with someone you trust.
steps: Think of someone who makes you feel seen/valued
Lower the bar: it's ok to just say 'hi' or 'thinking of you'
Send text or make brief call
You don't need a reason - connection IS the reason
Be honest if you're struggling (vulnerability)
Accept whatever response they can give

strategies.name: Join or Attend Community Activity
steps: Identify an activity aligned with your values or interests
Commit to attending once (no pressure to continue)
Go even if anxious - anxiety usually decreases after arrival
Focus on being present, not performing
One authentic connection is enough
Repeat weekly if it felt good
strategies.contraindications: May be difficult with social anxiety - start small

strategies.name: Intense Physical Exercise
strategies.description: Use vigorous movement to discharge emotional energy and regulate mood.
steps: Choose activity: running, dancing, boxing, etc.
Aim for intensity that increases heart rate
Duration: 20-40 minutes
Notice thoughts/emotions arising during exercise
Cool down with gentler movement
Notice mood shift after

strategies.name: Expressive Writing (Pennebaker Method)
strategies.description: Write about difficult emotions to process and release them.
steps: Set timer for 15-20 minutes
Write continuously about the emotional experience
Don't worry about grammar, spelling, or coherence
Write for yourself only - you can destroy it after
Let all feelings come out onto the page
Repeat for 3-4 consecutive days if possible
Notice: emotions often shift by day 3-4

strategies.name: Task Chunking and Prioritization
strategies.description: Break overwhelming tasks into manageable pieces.
steps: List all tasks causing overwhelm
Break each into smallest possible steps
Use Eisenhower Matrix: Urgent/Important
Choose ONE thing to do right now
Do only that one thing
Celebrate completion
Then choose next one thing

strategies.name: Permission to Say No / Set Boundaries
strategies.description: Practice declining requests that drain you to protect emotional energy.
steps: Notice feeling of obligation or resentment
Ask: Is this a 'should' or a 'want'?
Remember: 'No' is a complete sentence
Softer alternatives: 'Not right now' or 'Let me check my capacity'
Practice phrase: 'I appreciate the offer, but I need to decline'
Notice relief after setting boundary

strategies.name: TIPP Skills for Crisis
strategies.description: DBT skills to rapidly reduce intense arousal: Temperature, Intense exercise, Paced breathing, Paired muscle relaxation.
steps: T - Temperature: Splash cold water on face or hold ice cube
I - Intense exercise: 10 jumping jacks, run in place, vigorous movement
P - Paced breathing: Slow breathing to 5-6 breaths/minute
P - Paired muscle relaxation: Tense and release muscle groups
Use when emotional arousal is very high
Follow with gentler regulation once arousal decreases

strategies.name: Radical Acceptance (DBT)
strategies.description: Accept reality completely without fighting it - reduces suffering from resistance.
steps: Notice what you're fighting against
Observe the pain that resistance causes
Remind yourself: 'It is as it is'
Acceptance ≠ approval (you can accept AND work to change)
Let go of 'should' - replace with 'is'
Notice reduced suffering when you stop fighting reality

strategies.name: Values Clarification Exercise
strategies.description: Reconnect with personal values to guide decision-making and reduce shame.
steps: Write down 5-7 personal core values (what matters most to you)
For each value, write why it's important
Reflect: When did you last honor this value?
Distinguish: 'I acted against my values' vs 'I am bad'
Behavior can change - identity is more stable
Commit to one small action aligned with top value

strategies.name: Three Good Things (Gratitude Practice)
strategies.description: Shift attention to positive by noting three good things each day.
steps: Each evening, write down 3 good things from the day
They can be small: good coffee, kind word, sunshine
For each, write WHY it was good or how it made you feel
Notice shift from foreboding joy to genuine appreciation
Continue daily for at least 1 week for effect
